name: broadcast-underlay
detail: Configure the underlay in a LISP network to use a multicast group to send encapsulated broadcast packets and link local multicast packets.

params.name: multicast-ip
params.description: IP address of the multicast group that sends the encapsulated broadcast packets

usageSummary: Use this command to enable the broadcast functionality on the fabric edge node in a LISP network.  Ensure that this command is used in the router-lisp-service-ethernet mode or router-lisp-instance-service-ethernet mode.

name: database-mapping
detail: Configure an IPv4 or IPv6 endpoint identifier-to-routing locator (EID-to-RLOC) mapping relationship and an associated traffic policy for Location Identifier Separation Protocol (LISP).
syntax: database-mapping eid-prefix / prefix-length { locator-set RLOC-name [ proxy | default-etr | default-etr-route-map | route-tag | silent-host-detection ] | ipv6-interface interface-name | ipv4-interface interface-name | auto-discover-rlocs | limit } default-etr-route-map | route-tag | silent-host-detection ] | ipv6-interface interface-name | ipv4-interface interface-name | auto-discover-rlocs | limit }
noForm: no database-mapping eid-prefix / prefix-length { locator-set RLOC-name [ proxy | default-etr |

params.name: eid-prefix / prefix-length
params.description: IPv4 or IPv6 endpoint identifier prefix and length that is advertised by the router.

params.name: locator-set RLOC-name
params.description: Routing Locator (RLOC) associated with the value specified for the eid-prefix. Use the following keyword options for database mapping: • proxy: Enables configuration of static proxy database mapping. • default-etr: Enables configuration of default Egress Tunnel Router (ETR) database mapping. • route-tag route-tag: Monitors the RIB entry for a match with the route-tag specified. • default-etr-route-map route-map: Enables the route map to look for default-etr RIB route updates, and dynamically changes the locator set for this database mapping. • silent-host-detection: Enables silent host detection for hosts under the EID prefix.

params.name: ipv4 interface
params.description: IPv4 address and name of the interface that is used as the RLOC for the EID

params.name: interface-name
params.description: prefix.

params.name: ipv6 interface
params.description: IPv6 address and name of the interface that is used as the RLOC for the EID

params.name: interface-name
params.description: prefix.

params.name: auto-discover-rlocs
params.description: Configures the ETR to discover the locators of all the routers configured to function as both an ETR and an Ingress Tunnel Router (ITR)—such routers are referred to as xTRs—in the ETR LISP site when the site uses multiple xTRs, and each xTR is configured to use DHCP-learned locators or configured with only its own locators.

params.name: limit
params.description: Specifies the maximum size of local EID prefixes database.

usageSummary: In the LISP-instance-service configuration mode, the database-mapping command configures LISP database parameters with a specified IPv4 or IPv6 EID prefix block.  The locator is the IPv4 or IPv6 address of any interface used as the RLOC address for the eid-prefix assigned to the site but can also be the loopback address of the interface.  When a LISP site has multiple locators associated with the same EID prefix block, multiple database-mapping commands are used to configure all of the locators for a given EID prefix block.

name: dynamic-eid
detail: Create a dynamic End Point Identifier (EID) policy and enter the dynamic-eid configuration mode on an xTR.
usageSummary: To configure LISP mobility, create a dynamic-EID roaming policy that can be referenced by the lisp mobility interface command.  After you execute the dynamic-eid command, the referenced LISP dynamic-EID policy is created and the device goes to dynamic-EID configuration mode.  In this mode, all attributes that are associated with the referenced LISP dynamic-EID policy can be configured.

name: dynamic-eid detection multiple-addr
detail: Enable the detection of multiple IP addresses for a single MAC address.

params.name: bridged-vm
params.description: Enables specific features of bridge-mode virtual machines (VM).

usageSummary: The VMs on a wireless host are networked in a bridge mode.  Each VM has its own IP address that is associated with the host MAC address.  This leads to a situation where several IP addresses (one on each of the VMs) are associated with a single MAC address (of the host).

name: eid-record-provider
detail: Define an extranet policy table for the provider instance use the eid-record-provider command in the LISP Extranet configuration mode. To negate the EID-record-provider configuration.
syntax: eid-record-provider instance-id instance id { ipv4 address prefix | ipv6 address prefix } bidirectional bidirectional
noForm: no eid-record-provider instance-id instance id { ipv4 address prefix | ipv6 address prefix }

params.name: ipv4 address prefix
params.description: IPv4 EID prefixes to be leaked. Prefix specified in a.b.c.d/nn form.

params.name: ipv6 address prefix
params.description: IPv6 EID prefixes to be leaked. Prefix specified in X:X:X:X::X/<0-128> form.

params.name: bidirectional
params.description: Specifies that the extranet communication between the provider and subscriber EID prefixes are bidirectional.

name: eid-record-subscriber
detail: Define an extranet policy table for the subscriber instance.
syntax: eid-record-subscriber instance-id instance id { ipv4 address prefix | ipv6 address prefix } bidirectional bidirectional
noForm: no eid-record-subscriber instance-id instance id { ipv4 address prefix | ipv6 address prefix }

params.name: ipv4 address prefix
params.description: IPv4 EID prefixes to be leaked. Prefix specified in a.b.c.d/nn form.

params.name: ipv6 address prefix
params.description: IPv6 EID prefixes to be leaked. Prefix specified in X:X:X:X::X/<0-128> form.

params.name: bidirectional
params.description: Specifies that the extranet communication between the provider and subscriber EID prefixes are bidirectional.

name: eid-table
detail: Configure a Locator ID Separation Protocol (LISP) instance ID for association with a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) table or default table through which the endpoint identifier (EID) address space is reachable.
noForm: no eid-table { vrf-name | default | vrf vrf-name }

params.name: default
params.description: Selects the default (global) routing table for association with the configured instance-service.

params.name: vrf
params.description: Selects the named VRF table for association with the configured instance. vrf-name

usageSummary: Use this command only in the LISP Instance Service mode.  For Layer 3 (service ipv4 / service ipv6), a VRF table is associated with the instance-service.  For Layer 2 (service ethernet), a VLAN is associated with the instance-service.

name: encapsulation
detail: Configure the type of encapsulation of the data packets in the LISP network.

params.name: encapsulation lisp
params.description: Specifies LISP-based encapsulation

usageSummary: Use the encapsulation vxlan command in the LISP Service Ethernet mode to encapuslate Layer 2 packets.  Use the encapsulation vxlan command in the LISP Service IPv4 or LISP Service IPv6 mode to encapsulate the Layer 3 packets.

name: etr
detail: Configure a device as an Egress Tunnel Router (ETR) use the etr command in the LISP Instance Service mode or LISP Service submode. To remove the ETR functionality.
usageSummary: Use this command to enable a device to perform the ETR functionality.  A router configured as an ETR is also typically configured with database-mapping commands so that the ETR knows what endpoint identifier (EID)-prefix blocks and corresponding locators are used for the LISP site.  In addition, the ETR should be configured to register with a map server with the etr map-server command, or to use static LISP EID-to-routing locator (EID-to-RLOC) mappings with the map-cache command to participate in LISP networking.

name: etr map-server
detail: Configure a map server to be used by the Egress Tunnel Router (ETR) when configuring the EIDs.
noForm: no etr map-server map-server-address { key [ 0 | 6 | 7 ] authentication-key | proxy-reply

params.name: key
params.description: Specifies the key type.
Indicates that password is entered as clear text.
Indicates that password is in the AES encrypted form.
Indicates that password is a weak encrypted one.

params.name: authentication-key
params.description: The password used for computing the SHA-1 HMAC hash that is included in the header of the map-register message.

params.name: proxy-reply
params.description: Specifies that the map server answer the map-requests on behalf the ETR.

usageSummary: Use the etr map-server command to configure the locator of the map server to which the ETR will register for its EIDs.  The authentication key argument in the command syntax is a password that is used for a SHA-1 HMAC hash (included in the header of the map-register message).  The password used for the SHA-1 HMAC may be entered in unencrypted (cleartext) form or encrypted form.

name: extranet
detail: Enable inter-VRF communication in a LISP network.

params.name: name-extranet
params.description: Specifies the name of the extranet created.

name: extranet-config-from-transit
detail: Specify that extranet configuration must be learnt from the Transit Control Plane.
usageSummary: In multi-site deployment of an SD-Access fabric, an extranet policy is propagated from the transit map server map resolver (MSMR) to all the site map servers.  In such cases, run the extranet-config-from-transit command on the local map server to allow the extranet policy propagation from the transit MSMR to the site local map server.  After configuring this command, do not add or delete the policy on the local map server.

name: fast-detection
detail: Enable fast detection of endpoints.
usageSummary: LISP uses Switch Integrated Security Features (SISF) for host detection and by default the GUARD policy is installed.  GUARD policy follows a zero-trust process and provides security but affects the endpoint roaming latency.  To improve the wireless roaming performance, use the fast-detection command to enforce the SISF GLEAN policy.

name: first-packet-petr
detail: Prevent the loss of the first packet (and subsequent packets until map-cache is resolved).

params.name: remote-locator-set
params.description: Specifies a remote locator-set, which is a set of IP addresses of remote

params.name: fpetr-RLOC
params.description: devices, that connect to an external network or to networks across sites or to Data Center through remote or local sites.

usageSummary: The ITR or the fabric edge device drops the initial packets sent to it until it learns the destination EID reachability from the local MSMR.  To prevent the drop of the first packet, configure the first-packet-petr command on the local MSMR.  Configure the first-packet-petr command on the local map server to ensure that when the fabric edges boots up and resolves the 0/0 map-cache entry, it gets the first packet forwarding RLOCs.

name: import-database-publication locator-set
detail: Configure the import of map server publications to the database.
syntax: import database publication locator-set locator-set-name [ preserve-priority ]

params.name: preserve-priority
params.description: Specifies that the locator-set priority should be retained while importing the publication to the database.

usageSummary: In a LISP multisite topology wherein multiple fabric sites are connected through a transit, you can choose the point from where traffic can egress the multisite domain.  This is done by assigning a priority to the RLOCs when registering them with the map server.  An RLOC with a higher priority is chosen for the traffic to egress.

name: instance-id
detail: Create a LISP EID instance under the router-lisp configuration mode and enter the instance-id submode.

params.name: iid
params.description: Specifies the instance ID

usageSummary: Use the instance-id command to create a LISP EID instance to group multiple services.  Configuration under this instance applies to all the services underneath it.

name: ip pim lisp core-group-range
detail: Configure the core range of address of a Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) Source Specific Multicast (SSM) on a LISP sub-interface.
syntax: ip pim lisp core-group-range start-SSM-address range-size
noForm: no ip pim lisp core-group-range start-SSM-address range-size
usageSummary: Native multicast transport supports only PIM SSM in the underlay or the core.  Multicast transport uses a grouping mechanism to map the end-point identifiers (EID) entries to the RLOC space SSM group entries.  By default, the group range 232.

name: ip pim lisp transport multicast
detail: Enable multicast as the transport mechanism on LISP interface and sub-interface.
noForm: no ip pim lisp transport multicast

name: ip pim rp-address
detail: Configure the address of a Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) rendezvous point (RP) for a particular group.
syntax: ip pim [ vrf vrf-name ] rp-address rp-address [ access-list ]
noForm: no ip pim [ vrf vrf-name ] rp-address rp-address [ access-list ]

params.name: vrf
params.description: Specifies the multicast Virtual Private Network (VPN) routing and forwarding (VRF) instance.

params.name: vrf-name
params.description: Name assigned to the VRF. rp-address IP address of a router to be a PIM RP. This is a unicast IP address in four-part dotted-decimal notation. access-list Number or name of an access list that defines the multicast groups for which the RP should be used.

usageSummary: Use the ip pim rp-address command to statically define the RP address for multicast groups that are to operate in sparse mode or bidirectional mode.  You can configure the Cisco IOS XE software to use a single RP for more than one group.  The conditions specified by the access list determine the groups for which an RP can be used.

name: ip pim sparse mode
detail: Enable sparse mode of operation of Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) on an interface.
usageSummary: The NetFlow collect commands are used to configure nonkey fields for the flow monitor record and to enable capturing the values in the fields for the flow created with the record.  The values in nonkey fields are added to flows to provide additional information about the traffic in the flows.  A change in the value of a nonkey field does not create a new flow.

name: ipv4 multicast multitopology
detail: Enable Multicast-Specific RPF topology support for IP Multicast routing.

name: ip pim ssm
detail: Define the Source Specific Multicast (SSM) range of IP multicast addresses.
syntax: ip pim [ vrf vrf-name ] ssm { default | range access-list }
noForm: no ip pim [ vrf vrf-name ] ssm { default | range access-list }

params.name: vrf
params.description: Specifies the multicast Virtual Private Network (VPN) routing and forwarding (VRF) instance.

params.name: vrf-name
params.description: Name assigned to the VRF.

params.name: range
params.description: Specifies the standard IP access list number or name defining the SSM range. access-list

params.name: default2
params.description: Defines the SSM range access list to 232/8.

usageSummary: When an SSM range of IP multicast addresses is defined by the ip pim ssm command, no Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) Source-Active (SA) messages will be accepted or originated in the SSM range.

name: ipv4-interface Loopback affinity-id
detail: Configure an Affinity ID for a Locator.
syntax: ipv4-interface Loopback loopback-interface-id [ priority locator-priority weight locator-weight | affinity-id x-dimension [ , y-dimension ] ] | affinity-id x-dimension [ , y-dimension ] ]
noForm: no ipv4-interface Loopback loopback-interface-id [ priority locator-priority weight locator-weight

params.name: priority locator-priority
params.description: Configures a preferred Locator. Locator with a lower priority value takes preference. Values range from 0 to 255.

params.name: weight locator-weight
params.description: Configures the load-balance on the device. Values range from 0 to 100. affinity-id x-dimension[ ,y-dimension] Configures an Affinity ID, which is specified by the x dimension and an optional y dimension values.

usageSummary: First define a locator-set and then configure an affinity ID for its locator.  Affinity ID with its x and y dimensions identifies a particular site or region.  Affinity ID is a part of the locator information, like priority and weight.

name: itr
detail: Configure a device as an Ingress Tunnel Router (ITR) use the itr command in the LISP Service submode or LISP Instance Service mode. To remove the ITR functionality.
usageSummary: Use this command to enable a device to perform the ITR functionality.  A device configured as an ITR helps find the EID-to-RLOC mapping for all traffic that is destined to LISP-capable sites.

name: itr map-resolver
detail: Configure a device as a map resolver to be used by an Ingress Tunnel Router (ITR) when sending map-requests.
noForm: no itr [ map-resolver map-address ] prefix-list prefix-list-name
usageSummary: Use this command to enable a device to perform the ITR map-resolver functionality.  A device configured as a Map Resolver accepts encapsulated Map-Request messages from ITRs, decapsulates those messages, and then forwards the messages to the Map Server responsible for the egress tunnel routers (ETRs) that are authoritative for the requested EIDs.  In a multi-site environment, the site border relies on Map Resolver prefix-list to determine whether to query the transit site MSMR or site MSMR.

name: locator default-set
detail: Mark a locator-set as default.

params.name: rloc-set-name
params.description: Name of locator-set that is set as default.

usageSummary: The locator-set configured as default with the locator default-set command applies to all services and instances.

name: locator-set
detail: Specify a locator-set and enter the locator-set configuration mode.
usageSummary: You must first define the locator-set before referring to it.

name: map-cache
detail: Configure a static endpoint identifier (EID) to routing locator (RLOC) (EID-to-RLOC) mapping relationship.
syntax: map-cache destination-eid-prefix/prefix-len { ipv4-address { priority priority weight weight } | ipv6-address | map-request | native-forward } } | ipv6-address | map-request | native-forward }
noForm: no map-cache destination-eid-prefix/prefix-len { ipv4-address { priority priority weight weight

params.name: destination-eid-prefix/prefix-len
params.description: Destination IPv4 or IPv6 EID-prefix/prefix-length. The slash is required in the syntax.

params.name: ipv4-address priority priority
params.description: IPv4 Address of loopback interface. Associated with this locator address

params.name: weight weight
params.description: is a priority and weight that are used to define traffic policies when multiple RLOCs are defined for the same EID-prefix block. Note Lower priority locator takes preference.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: IPv6 Address of loopback interface.

params.name: map-request
params.description: Send map-request for LISP destination EID

params.name: native-forward
params.description: Natively forward packets that match this map-request.

usageSummary: The first use of this command is to configure an Ingress Tunnel Router (ITR) with a static IPv4 or IPv6 EID-to-RLOC mapping relationship and its associated traffic policy.  For each entry, a destination EID-prefix block and its associated locator, priority, and weight are entered.  The value in the EID-prefix/prefix-length argument is the LISP EID-prefix block at the destination site.

name: map-cache extranet
detail: Install all configured extranet prefixes into map-cache.
usageSummary: To support inter-VRF communication, use the map-cache extranet command on the Map Server Map Resolver (MSMR).  This command generates map requests for all fabric destinations.  Use this command in the service-ipv4 or service-ipv6 mode under the extranet instance.

name: prefix-list
detail: Define a named LISP prefix set and to enter the LISP prefix-list configuration mode.
usageSummary: Use the prefix-list command to configure an IPV4 or IPv6 prefix list.  This command places the router in prefix-list configuration mode, in which you can define IPv4 prefix list, or IPv6 prefix list.  Use the exit-prefix-list command to exit the prefix-list-configuration mode.

name: route-export destinations-summary
detail: Export the LISP destination summary routes into the Routing Information Base (RIB).

params.name: route-tag route-tag-value
params.description: A tag that is assigned to the exported RIB entry. The route-tag-value ranges between 0 to 4294967295.

usageSummary: When you configure the route-export destinations-summary route-tag route-tag-value command, the static endpoint ID to routing locator (EID-to-RLOC) mappings are exported to RIB as routes with a specified route tag.  If you use this command in the LISP Service mode, all the EID instances that are enabled for Layer 3 services export the map-cache mappings to the RIB.

name: route-import database
detail: Configure the import of Routing Information Base (RIB) routes to define local endpoint identifier (EID) prefixes for database entries and associate them with a locator set.
syntax: route-import database { bgp | connected | eigrp | isis | maximum-prefix | ospf | ospfv3 | rip | static } { [ route-map ] locator-set locator-set-name proxy } rip | static } { [ route-map ] locator-set locator-set-name proxy }
noForm: no route-import database { bgp | connected | eigrp | isis | maximum-prefix | ospf | ospfv3 |

params.name: bgp
params.description: Border Gateway Protocol. Imports RIB routes into LISP using BGP protocol.

params.name: connected
params.description: Connected routing protocol

params.name: eigrp
params.description: Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol. Imports RIB routes into LISP using EIGRP protocol.

params.name: isis
params.description: ISO IS-IS. Imports RIB routes into LISP using IS-IS protocol.

params.name: ospf
params.description: Open Shortest Path First

params.name: ospfv3
params.description: Open Shortest Path First version 3

params.name: maximum-prefix
params.description: Configures the maximum number of prefixes to pick up from the RIB.

params.name: rip
params.description: Routing Information Protocol

params.name: static
params.description: Defines static routes.

params.name: locator-set
params.description: Specifies the Locator Set to be used with created database mapping entries. locator-set-name

params.name: proxy
params.description: Enables the dynamic import of RIB route as proxy database mapping.

usageSummary: Use the route-import database command with the proxy option to enable the dynamic import of RIB route as proxy database mapping.  When RIB import is in use, the corresponding RIB map-cache import, using route-import map-cache command must also be configured, else the inbound site traffic will not pass the LISP eligibility check due to the presence of RIB route.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.

name: service
detail: Create a configuration template for all instance-service instantiations of a particular service.

params.name: service ipv4
params.description: Enables Layer 3 network services for the IPv4 address family.

params.name: service ipv6
params.description: Enables Layer 3 network services for the IPv6 address family. service ethernet Enables Layer 2 network services.

usageSummary: The service command creates a service instance under the instance-id and enters the instance-service mode.  You cannot configure service ethernet for the same instance where service ipv4 or service ipv6 is configured.

name: sgt
detail: Configure the propagation of security group tag (SGT) information through the LISP packets.

params.name: distribution
params.description: SGT information is distributed through the LISP packets.

name: show lisp instance-id
detail: Display the summary information related to a given LISP instance configuration for an address family.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv6 show lisp instance-id instance-id ethernet
usageSummary: Use this command on a LISP-based fabric device to display the summary information about the map resolver, map server, map cache, database, publications, and other configurations.  Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 command to see a summary of the LISP instance configuration for the IPv4 address family.  Use the show lisp instance-id ipv6 command to see a summary of the LISP instance configuration for the IPv6 address family.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 database
detail: Display the operational status of the IPv4 address family and the database mappings on a device.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 database [ silent-host-detection ]

params.name: silent-host-detection
params.description: (Optional) Displays the silent host detection database entry on the tunnel router (xTR).

name: show lisp instance-id ipv6 database
detail: Display the operational status of the IPv6 address family and the database mappings on a device.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv6 database [ silent-host-detection ]

params.name: silent-host-detection
params.description: (Optional) Displays the silent host detection database entry on the xTR.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 publication config-propagation
detail: Display the config-propagation type of LISP-mapping notifications or publications for extranet policy.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 publication config-propagation [ detail | ipv4-prefix ]

params.name: detail
params.description: EID prefix details from all publications ipv4-prefix IPv4 EID prefix of a particular publication

usageSummary: Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 publication config-propagation detail command on the border node to see a detailed report of all the extranet policy publications.  Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 publication config-propagation ipv4-prefix command to view the extranet policy publication for the particular EID prefix specified by ipv4-prefix.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 publisher config-propagation
detail: Display the config-propagation type of LISP publications that a publisher propagates.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 publisher config-propagation [ ipv4-address | ipv6-address ]

params.name: ipv4-address
params.description: IPv4 address of the publisher

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: IPv6 address of the publisher

usageSummary: Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 publisher config-propagation command on the border node to see a report of all the publishers.  Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 publisher config-propagation ip-address command to view the information for the publisher that is specified by the IP address.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 map-cache
detail: Display the IPv4 end point identifier (EID) to the Resource Locator (RLOC) cache mapping on an ITR.

params.name: destination-EID
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the IPv4 destination end point identifier (EID) for which the EID-to-RLOC mapping is displayed. destination-EID-prefix (Optional) Specifies the IPv4 destinationEID prefix (in the form of a.b.c.d/nn) for which to display the mapping.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed EID-to-RLOC cache mapping information.

usageSummary: This command is used to display the current dynamic and static IPv4 EID-to-RLOC map-cache entries.  When no IPv4 EID or IPv4 EID prefix is specified, summary information is listed for all current dynamic and static IPv4 EID-to-RLOC map-cache entries.  When an IPv4 EID or IPv4 EID prefix is included, information is listed for the longest-match lookup in the cache.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv6 map-cache
detail: Display the IPv6 end point identifier (EID) to the Resource Locator (RLOC) cache mapping on an ITR.

params.name: destination-EID
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the IPv4 destination end point identifier (EID) for which the EID-to-RLOC mapping is displayed. destination-EID-prefix (Optional) Specifies the IPv4 destination EID prefix (in the form of a.b.c.d/nn) for which to display the mapping.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed EID-to-RLOC cache mapping information.

usageSummary: This command is used to display the current dynamic and static IPv6 EID-to-RLOC map-cache entries.  When no IPv6 EID or IPv6 EID prefix is specified, summary information is listed for all current dynamic and static IPv4 EID-to-RLOC map-cache entries.  When an IPv6 EID or IPv6 EID prefix is included, information is listed for the longest-match lookup in the cache.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 server
detail: Display the Location Identifier Separation Protocol (LISP) site registration information.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 server [ EID-address | EID-prefix | detail | name | rloc | summary | silent-host-detection ]

params.name: EID-address
params.description: (Optional) Site registration information for this end point.

params.name: EID-prefix
params.description: (Optional) Site registration information for this IPv4 EID prefix.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed site information.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Displays site registration information for the named site.

params.name: rloc
params.description: (Optional) Displays the routing locator endpoint identifier (RLOC-EID) instance membership details.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays summary information for each site. silent-host-detection (Optional) Displays the silent host detection registration information on the Map Server Map Resolver (MSMR).

usageSummary: When a host is detected by the tunnel router (xTR), it registers the host with the map server.  Use the show lisp instance-id ipv4 server command to see the site registration details.  TCP registrations display the port number, whereas UDP registrations do not display the port number.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv6 server
detail: Display the Location Identifier Separation Protocol (LISP) site registration information.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv6 server [ EID-address | EID-prefix | detail | name | rloc | summary | silent-host-detection ]

params.name: EID-address
params.description: (Optional) Site registration information for this end point.

params.name: EID-prefix
params.description: (Optional) Site registration information for this IPv6 EID prefix.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed site information.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Displays the site registration information for the named site.

params.name: rloc
params.description: (Optional) Displays the routing locator endpoint identifier (RLOC-EID) instance membership details.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays summary information for each site. silent-host-detection (Optional) Displays the silent host detection registration information on the Map Server Map Resolver (MSMR).

usageSummary: When a host is detected by the tunnel router (xTR), it registers the host with the map server.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv4 statistics
detail: Display Locator/ID Separation Protocol (LISP) IPv4 address-family packet count statistics.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv4 statistics
usageSummary: This command is used to display IPv4 LISP statistics related to packet encapsulations, de-encapsulations, map requests, map replies, map registers, and other LISP-related packets.  The following is a sample output of the show lisp instance-id 4099 ipv4 statistics command: device# show lisp instance-id 4099 ipv4 statistics LISP EID Statistics for instance ID 4099 - last cleared: never Control Packets: Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 Map-Requests in (5 sec/1 min/5 min): 0/0/0 Encapsulated Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 RLOC-probe Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 SMR-based Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 Extranet SMR cross-IID Map-Requests in: 0 Map-Requests expired on-queue/no-reply 0/0 Map-Resolver Map-Requests forwarded: 0 Map-Server Map-Requests forwarded: 0 Map-Reply records in/out: 0/0 Authoritative records in/out: 0/0 Non-authoritative records in/out: 0/0 Negative records in/out: 0/0 RLOC-probe records in/out: 0/0 Map-Server Proxy-Reply records out: 0 WLC Map-Subscribe records in/out: 2/2 Map-Subscribe failures in/out: 0/0 WLC Map-Unsubscribe records in/out: 1/1 Map-Unsubscribe failures in/out: 0/0 Map-Register records in/out: 11/11 Map-Registers in (5 sec/1 min/5 min): 0/0/0 Map-Server AF disabled: 0 Not valid site eid prefix: 2 Authentication failures: 0 Disallowed locators: 0 Miscellaneous: 0 WLC Map-Register records in/out: 0/0 WLC AP Map-Register in/out: 0/0 WLC Client Map-Register in/out: 0/0 Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: show lisp instance-id ipv6 statistics
detail: Display Locator/ID Separation Protocol (LISP) IPv6 address-family packet count statistics.
syntax: show lisp instance-id instance-id ipv6 statistics

params.name: This
params.description: command does not have any keywords or arguments.

usageSummary: This command is used to display IPv6 LISP statistics related to packet encapsulations, de-encapsulations, map requests, map replies, map registers, and other LISP-related packets.  The following is a sample output of the show lisp instance-id 4099 ipv6 statistics command : device# show lisp instance-id 4099 ipv6 statistics LISP EID Statistics for instance ID 4099 - last cleared: never Control Packets: Map-Requests in/out: 2/2 Map-Requests in (5 sec/1 min/5 min): 0/0/0 Encapsulated Map-Requests in/out: 2/2 RLOC-probe Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 SMR-based Map-Requests in/out: 0/0 Extranet SMR cross-IID Map-Requests in: 0 Map-Requests expired on-queue/no-reply 0/0 Map-Resolver Map-Requests forwarded: 0 Map-Server Map-Requests forwarded: 0 Map-Reply records in/out: 2/2 Authoritative records in/out: 2/2 Non-authoritative records in/out: 0/0 Negative records in/out: 0/0 RLOC-probe records in/out: 0/0 Map-Server Proxy-Reply records out: 0 WLC Map-Subscribe records in/out: 2/2 Map-Subscribe failures in/out: 0/0 WLC Map-Unsubscribe records in/out: 1/1 Map-Unsubscribe failures in/out: 0/0 Map-Register records in/out: 9/9 Map-Registers in (5 sec/1 min/5 min): 0/0/0 Map-Server AF disabled: 0 Not valid site eid prefix: 2 Authentication failures: 0 Disallowed locators: 0 Miscellaneous: 0 WLC Map-Register records in/out: 0/0 WLC AP Map-Register in/out: 0/0 WLC Client Map-Register in/out: 0/0 Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: show lisp prefix-list
detail: Display the LISP prefix-list information.

params.name: name-prefix-list
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the prefix-list whose information is displayed.

name: show lisp session
detail: Display the current list of reliable transport sessions in the fabric.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays transport session inforamtion for all the sessions. established (Optional) Displays transport session information for established connections.

usageSummary: The show lisp session command displays only those sessions that are in Up or Down state.  Use the show lisp session all command to see all sessions in any state.  The following is a sample output of the command show lisp session on an MSMR: device# show lisp session Sessions for VRF default, total: 4, established: 2 Peer State Up/Down In/Out Users 172.

name: use-petr
detail: Configure a router to use an IPv4 or IPv6 Locator/ID Separation Protocol (LISP) Proxy Egress Tunnel Router (PETR).
syntax: use-petr locator-address [ priority priority weight weight ]
noForm: no use-petr locator-address [ priority priority weight weight ]

params.name: locator-address
params.description: The name of locator-set that is set as default. priority priority (Optional) Specifies the priority (value between 0 and 255) assigned to this PETR. A lower value indicates a higher priority.

params.name: weight weight
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the percentage of traffic to be load-shared (value between 0 and 100).

usageSummary: Use the use-petr command to enable an Ingress Tunnel Router (ITR) or Proxy Ingress Tunnel Router (PITR) to use IPv4 Proxy Egress Tunnel Router (PETR) services.  When the use of PETR services is enabled, instead of natively forwarding LISP endpoint identifier (EID) (source) packets destined to non-LISP sites, these packets are LISP-encapsulated and forwarded to the PETR.  Upon receiving these packets, the PETR decapsulates them and then forwards them natively toward the non-LISP destination.
